= Issue Templates
:description: Standardized issue reporting for paint-type

This directory contains GitHub issue templates that provide structure and guidance
for reporting bugs, requesting features, asking questions, and submitting other
types of issues to the paint-type repository.

== Purpose

Issue templates serve several important purposes:

* *Standardization*: Ensure all issues contain necessary information for triage
* *Efficiency*: Reduce back-and-forth by collecting required data upfront
* *Guidance*: Help contributors understand what information is needed
* *Categorization*: Automatically apply appropriate labels based on issue type
* *Quality*: Improve the overall quality of issue submissions

== Contents by Functional Grouping

=== Issue Type Templates

* `bug_report.yml`:: Template for reporting bugs and defects. Collects information about:
  - Expected vs. actual behavior
  - Steps to reproduce
  - Environment details (OS, version, etc.)
  - Screenshots or error messages
  - Severity and impact assessment

* `feature_request.yml`:: Template for requesting new features. Collects information about:
  - Feature description and use case
  - Proposed solution or API
  - Alternatives considered
  - Priority and complexity assessment

* `documentation.yml`:: Template for documentation requests and improvements. Collects:
  - Missing or unclear documentation
  - Suggested improvements
  - Location of relevant code

* `question.yml`:: Template for general questions and support requests. Collects:
  - Question details
  - What has been tried
  - Relevant context

* `custom.yml`:: Custom template for other issue types not covered above.

=== Configuration

* `config.yml`:: GitHub issue template configuration. Defines:
  - Which templates are available
  - Default template selection
  - Template ordering in the UI

== Purpose of Each Template

=== bug_report.yml

The bug report template is designed to collect all information needed to:

* Reproduce the issue consistently
* Understand the impact and severity
* Prioritize the fix appropriately
* Assign to the correct maintainer or team

Without complete bug reports, maintainers spend significant time requesting
additional information, delaying resolution.

=== feature_request.yml

The feature request template helps:

* Clarify the requested functionality
* Understand the motivation and use cases
* Evaluate feasibility and complexity
* Prioritize against other requests
* Design the feature properly

Well-structured feature requests make it easier to assess and plan new functionality.

=== documentation.yml

The documentation template helps:

* Identify documentation gaps
* Understand what users need to know
* Prioritize documentation improvements
* Track documentation-related work

Good documentation reduces support burden and improves user experience.

=== question.yml

The question template helps:

* Provide context for the question
* Understand what the user has tried
* Route to the appropriate maintainer
* Track frequently asked questions

Many questions can be answered more efficiently with proper context.

== Template Structure

All templates follow a consistent structure:

1. *Name*: Clear, descriptive title
2. *Description*: What the template is for and when to use it
3. *Labels*: Automatic labels to apply
4. *Assignees*: Optional default assignees
5. *Body*: The form fields to collect information

== Best Practices

When creating or modifying templates:

* Keep forms concise but comprehensive
* Use clear, descriptive field names
* Group related fields together
* Use dropdowns for standardized options
* Include helpful descriptions and examples
* Mark required fields appropriately
